text:
Hisoka's Love × Showdown × Gon's Resolve (ヒソカの愛×決着×ゴンの本気, Hisoka no ai × Kecchaku × Gon no Honki) is the 42 nd episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime. It first aired on October 21 st , 2000.

Overview

Gon and Hisoka continue their fight. Hisoka attaches "Bungee Gum" to Gon's face and pulls him in to punch. The match goes in Hisoka's favor. Hisoka has a spider tattoo on his back, having some connection with the Phantom Troupe. Machi tells him the boss won't be pleased if he doesn't attend the Phantom Troupe meeting.

Synopsis

Machi walks into the stands to watch Gon and Hisoka's fight. After returning his badge from Gon, Hisoka and Gon's match continues.

Hisoka asks Gon about his Nen training and can guess that Gon is an Enhancer accurately. When Gon questions how he could know that, Hisoka outlines his Personality Test for determining someone's Nen type, which Wing backs from the stands when Zushi asks him about it.

Hisoka charges at Gon and lands several hits on him, although Gon can always land on his feet and keep going. Gon continues to run from Hisoka, unable to devise a good plan of attack.

In a flashback to just after Hisoka's match with Kastro, Wing shows Gon and Killua a videotape of Hisoka and Kastro's fight. The two boys use Gyo to try and view Hisoka's Nen ability on the video and claim to see a bunch of threads coming off of Hisoka's left hand; Wing confirms this is Hisoka's Bungee Gum ability.

In the present, Gon becomes wary of Hisoka's ability during their match, but Hisoka has already attached his Bungee Gum to Gon's cheek. Hisoka pulls Gon towards him by the gum, lands two hits on him, and Gon falls to the ground. Hisoka tells Gon to get up, and Gon struggles to get to his feet but manages it. Despite Hisoka being in the lead 6-2, the crowd still cheers for Gon.

Togashi, somehow managing to get a seat next to the announcer of the fight, explains how Hisoka's ability is based on a brand of gum from his poor childhood. However, Togashi is swiftly kicked out of the announcer's co-seat, and the fight continues. 

Hisoka offers Gon a free punch on him if Gon can say when Hisoka's gum was first attached to him in the fight. Hisoka gives him three different multiple-choice options, and Gon guesses wrong, only for Hisoka to reveal the answer is an entirely different fourth option.

After deciding to ignore Hisoka's ability, Gon charges at Hisoka and hits him many times, none of which Hisoka deflects or dodges. Instead, Hisoka happily takes Gon's attacks for the time being. Eventually, though, Hisoka switches up and pulls Gon in with his gum to land several hits on him. Despite all of Gon's successful hits and Gon continuing to get back on his feet, the referee is somewhat biased against Gon, and the score quickly becomes 9-4 for Hisoka.

Hisoka tricks Gon into looking into the audience, and while Gon is distracted, Hisoka hits him with a piece of stone from the flooring, knocking Gon down and winning Hisoka the match. 

Before leaving, Hisoka tells Gon that Gon has genuinely improved, but still has a long way to go. He promises Gon that the next fight they have will be outside of Heavens Arena and will be to the death. 

Back in his room, Hisoka exits the shower and towels his hair. Machi stands by his window, waiting for him with a message. She tells him that the next Phantom Troupe meeting in Yorknew City on August 30 th is mandatory, and Hisoka's spider tattoo on his bare back is revealed. Machi warns him not to miss the meeting and then leaves. Once alone in his room, Hisoka removes the spider tattoo from his back, showing it's fake.
